steps.title: Count tall trees
steps.content: Count trees that are $20$ feet or taller. Birch: $4\cdot 9=36$. Maple: $7\cdot 6=42$.

steps.title: Form the probability
steps.content: Given the tree is at least $20$ feet tall, the total is $36+42=78$. The maple probability is $\frac{42}{78}=\frac{7}{13}$. The answer is C.
